title: Immunolabeling of a Whole Mouse Brain
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dai40","text":"The mouse brains prepared using this protocol have sparse viral labeling of target neuron populations with genetically encoded fluorescent proteins (FPs). Delipidation ensures that whole brain samples become optically clear and more accessible to antibody labeling. However, the signal from endogenous FPs is often dimmed or quenched during organic solvent delipidation. Tissue expansion also dilutes the concentration of fluorophores. Immunolabeling is necessary to amplify signals of FPs for high resolution, multi-scale imaging, of delipidated whole brain samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"fbt3c","text":"If performing the Whole Mouse Brain Delipidation, Immunolabeling, and Expansion Microscopy protocol, start with a brain that has been delipidated using the Tetrahydrofuran and Dichloromethane Delipidation of a Whole Mouse Brain and Aqueous (SBiP) Delipidation of a Whole Mouse Brain protocols. This protocol can also be used to immunolabel brains delipidated using LifeCanvas clearing, iDISCO, or other similar clearing methods. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":227,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":18,"length":72},{"key":1,"offset":156,"length":71},{"key":2,"offset":232,"length":50}],"data":{}},{"key":"736f8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4405E900403211F19C300A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/whole-mouse-brain-delipidation-immunolabeling-and-n92ldpwjxl5b/v1"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2A0B2A54403111F1802C0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/tetrahydrofuran-and-dichloromethane-delipidation-o-36wgqj1kxvk5/v2"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"51F552C8403111F1802C0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/aqueous-sbip-delipidation-of-a-whole-mouse-brain-n2bvj81mwgk5/v2"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6ebcv","text":"PTxw contains harmful chemicals like 5% Sodium Azide. Use appropriate gloves and PPE while handling, avoid contact with skin or clothing, and dispose of it properly in a hazardous waste stream. steps: Transfer the brain to a small 4 mL Shorty Vial containing diluted primary antibody in ~ 4 mL PTxw and incubate with rocking, a nutator, or shaker at 4 °C for 11 days. 15 μg/brain for primary antibody 30 μg/brain for primary conjugated antibody

Transfer the brain to a 20 mL Liquid Scintillation vial and wash with PTxw, rocking on a nutating mixer or shaker at Room temperature , and replacing solution for each step: PTxw for 01:00:00 PTxw for 02:00:00 PTxw for 04:00:00 PTxw Overnight PTxw for 24:00:00 PTxw for 24:00:00 PTxw for 24:00:00, +
Using a 20 mL glass vial, wash the brain with PTxw, rotating at Room temperature Overnight .

Spin down the desired secondary antibody in a centrifuge set to 10.000 rpm, °C, for 00:01:00 and use the supernatant for immunolabeling. Transfer the brain to a small 4 mL Shorty vial containing diluted secondary antibody in ~ 4 mL PTxw and incubate with rocking at 4 °C for 11 days. 30 μg/brain for secondary antibody

Prepare a new solution of PTxw containing 200mM NaCl and 0.1% Tween, and only use this new solution for the remaining washes. Transfer brain to a 20 mL Liquid Scintillation vial and wash with new PTxw, rocking at Room temperature and replacing solution for each step: PTxw for 01:00:00 PTxw for 02:00:00 PTxw for 04:00:00 PTxw Overnight PTxw for 24:00:00 PTxw for 24:00:00 PTxw for 24:00:00, +
Wash the brain with 1X PBS, rocking at Room temperature Overnight .
Store the immunolabeled brain in 0.01% PBS Azide at 4 °C
Start with a whole mouse brain perfused with 4% PFA, post-fixed, and stored in PBS in a 20 mL vial.
creator: Naveen Ouellette
